definition: 0DTE options (~45-50% of SPX volume, up from <5% in 2019), covered call ETF AUM (~$5B to ~$80B+), and systematic vol-selling (~$400-600B notional) have structurally altered equity vol production. These are non-cyclical structural shifts that have changed the distribution shape rather than just the level, creating structural compression that is itself fragile and tends to reverse simultaneously.
evidence: 0DTE options at ~45-50% of SPX volume up from <5% in 2019
Covered call ETF AUM from ~$5B to ~$80B+
All debates acknowledge these as non-cyclical structural shifts
Both pair_0 and pair_3 debates agree that equity-specific suppressors are fragile and tend to reverse simultaneously
open_questions: At what point does the vol-selling complex become self-referential and collapse?
How do autocallable product flows interact with 0DTE dynamics?
Can the structural compression persist through a genuine macro shock?
